title: Skip empty hooks directories by default
body:
We should not consider empty hooks directories because they could lead
into inappropriate error messages. As a background information, we would
like to be able to specify empty hooks directories in CRI-O without further
processing of the hooks-dir configuration string slice.

Relates to https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o/issues/3179

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *saschagrunert* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Why are we adding empty-string hooks directories? I'm really confused as to what's going on here - this seems somewhat questionable?
If we should be skipping empty directories, code LGTM.  Interested in hearing the answer to @mheon's question.

I believe the code search 
/usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d
/etc/containers/oci/hooks.d
If either one of these is empty then we should ignore it.
@saschagrunert Is this the issue?
I'm fine with not erroring on hooks directories that are specified, but don't exist, but this seems to be ignoring *the empty string* - which is very different, and looks like invalid input?
If it's just directories that are specified not existing, we should already catch that lower in the file?

> I'm fine with not erroring on hooks directories that are specified, but don't exist, but this seems to be ignoring _the empty string_ - which is very different, and looks like invalid input?

Sorry for the confusion. Yeah it's about invalid input. I played around with CRI-O (using this part of libpod) and had multiple combinations of CLI and config parameters which more or less resulted in passing a `[]string{""}` slice to this function. I'll close it for now and check if we can solve it in CRI-O in a more meaningful way.
